evidence: The paper explicitly states: 'Variable importance is calculated as the Z-score of the mean decrease in classification accuracy following the removal of this variable from the model.' This describes permutation importance (MDA), not impurity-based importance. Although the paper ranks variables by importance ('The most important variables...were intervertebral disc herniation...') and reports specific scores, the impurity-importance bias is irrelevant because they use permutation importance through Boruta, which is a robust selection method. Validation occurs via 10% hold-out test set with five-fold cross-validation and univariate statistics on selected variables.
